objective: Implement the local model runtime and guardrail lane slice from epic #224 by documenting Mac/Windows runtime inventory, adding no-payload health probes, defining fail-closed PII routing boundaries, and resolving the stale Windows VRAM assumption without granting autonomous write authority.
scope_boundary: Inventory Mac hardware and local inference availability without sending payloads to model endpoints.
Probe Windows Ollama health through metadata-only `/api/tags` reachability and model-list checks.
Document Mac M5 Pro 48 GB memory budgets for always-resident guardrail/intent models and on-demand worker/critic models.
Resolve the Windows hardware conflict by removing the unverified 16 GB VRAM assertion from the active standard and marking Windows VRAM as unverified until direct host evidence exists.
Keep Windows LAN-only fallback/batch/health infrastructure unless a later issue expands authority.
Ensure PII routing fails closed when patterns or local guardrail readiness are unavailable.
out_of_scope: Installing, starting, or downloading local models.
Sending real prompts, PII, or synthetic payloads to cloud, Windows, or local model endpoints.
Granting autonomous packet dispatch or write authority.
Adding Cloudflare/off-LAN Windows exposure, Wake-on-LAN, or remote host management.
Changing local-ai-machine runtime code from this governance slice.
research_summary: Local checks on 2026-04-17 show this Mac is a MacBook Pro Mac17,8 with Apple M5 Pro, 18 cores, and 48 GB memory. `mlx_lm` is importable, while `ollama` was not found on PATH in this shell. The Windows Ollama endpoint `http://172.17.227.49:11434/api/tags` timed out from the Mac, so the Windows host cannot be treated as live or hardware-verified in this slice. Existing STANDARDS.md still claimed a Windows workstation with 64 GB RAM and 16 GB VRAM; issue #224 notes the operator report conflicts with that. This slice resolves the conflict conservatively: Windows has 64 GB RAM by prior/operator report, no verified discrete VRAM in governance evidence, and remains LAN-only fallback/batch/health infrastructure until direct hardware telemetry is captured. Health probes are metadata-only and PII guardrail checks use local pattern files only.

sprints.name: Runtime Inventory Probe
sprints.goal: Add no-payload runtime inventory and guardrail health probes.
tasks: Add a stdlib runtime inventory script under `scripts/lam/`.
Report Mac hardware, MLX availability, local Ollama CLI availability, Windows Ollama metadata reachability, and local PII pattern readiness.
Ensure probes do not send prompt payloads to model endpoints.
Add tests for fail-closed PII behavior and Windows timeout handling.
acceptance_criteria: Health probes report installed/running model state without sending sensitive payloads.
PII guardrail readiness fails closed when patterns cannot load.
Windows timeout/unreachable state does not route work.

sprints.name: Runtime Placement Docs
sprints.goal: Document Mac/Windows model placement and routing boundaries.
tasks: Add `docs/runbooks/local-model-runtime.md` with memory budgets and model placement.
Add `docs/runbooks/windows-ollama.md` with current Windows verification status and LAN-only role.
Update STANDARDS.md to replace the stale Windows 16 GB VRAM assertion with unverified hardware status.
acceptance_criteria: Mac M5 Pro 48 GB memory budget is documented.
Windows hardware conflict is resolved conservatively before model placement is finalized.

sprints.name: Governance Evidence
sprints.goal: Register the runtime inventory and complete reviewed closeout.
tasks: Update feature, service, and data dictionary docs.
Add PDCAR, cross-review, and closeout artifacts.
Refresh graph/wiki and compendium after closeout.
acceptance_criteria: Registry docs describe runtime inventory outputs and PII fail-closed contract.
Alternate-family review passes or findings are resolved.
Closeout hook completes and compendium is current.

specialist_reviews.focus: No-payload probes, PII fail-closed behavior, conservative Windows hardware resolution, and documentation accuracy.
specialist_reviews.summary: The slice adds inventory/reporting and documentation only. It does not install models, send prompts, or grant autonomous routing authority.

alternate_model_review.summary: Alternate-family review required for Tier 1 model-routing and PII boundary changes. Findings are recorded in the #228 cross-review artifact and must be resolved or explicitly accepted before merge.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Implement #228 runtime inventory, no-payload health probes, local/Windows runbooks, conservative STANDARDS update, tests, review, and closeout only.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Build probe and docs, run tests and validators, obtain alternate-family review, close out, open PR, and merge after CI passes.

material_deviation_rules: If Windows hardware telemetry cannot be read directly, record it as unverified and do not finalize Windows model placement beyond LAN-only fallback/batch/health.
If any probe would send prompt content, remove or defer that probe.
If PII pattern files are missing or malformed, guardrail readiness must report fail-closed.
If local model installation is needed, defer to a separate operator-run issue.
approved_by: operator instruction: proceed, loop through, no HITL
